entities.description: A short-duration pigeonpea cultivar used in the study

entities.name: TERMINAL DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Water stress occurring during the terminal growth stage

entities.description: Treatment involving partial removal of flowers and pods to analyze yield components

entities.name: FOLIAR FERTILIZATION
entities.description: Application of nutrient solutions containing N, P, K, and S to the leaves

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of seed produced, specifically measured for top-3 nodes

entities.name: RAINFED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Growing conditions relying on natural rainfall

entities.name: IRRIGATED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Growing conditions with supplemental water application

entities.name: POD DENSITY
entities.description: The number of pods per unit area or plant

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The physical dimensions or weight of individual seeds

entities.name: SEEDS PER POD
entities.description: The number of seeds contained within a single pod

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: Nutrient N used in foliar fertilization treatments

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: Nutrient P used in foliar fertilization treatments

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: Nutrient K used in foliar fertilization treatments

entities.name: SULFUR
entities.description: Nutrient S used in foliar fertilization treatments

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE CONDITION
entities.description: The availability of water in the soil affecting plant growth

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE SINK CAPACITY
entities.description: The plant's capacity to utilize resources for reproductive growth

entities.name: NUTRIENT SUPPLY
entities.description: The availability of N, P, K, and S to the plant

entities.name: DROUGHT RESISTANCE MECHANISM
entities.description: Biological processes that allow the plant to withstand water stress

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: General condition of insufficient water availability for the plant
